# 🚢 Optical Ship Detection with Deep Learning (YOLO)

### High-Resolution Satellite Imagery | GeoAI Tutorial

This repository provides a **hands-on tutorial for ship detection in high-resolution optical satellite imagery using deep learning (YOLO)**.

👉 **Open the notebook:**\
[View Optical Ship Detection Notebook](https://github.com/Tnecniv-Teikram/ship_detection_optical/blob/main/ship_detection.ipynb)

***

## 📌 Overview

This tutorial introduces a **modern GeoAI workflow** for detecting ships in optical (RGB) satellite imagery.

Key highlights:

* Uses **YOLO (You Only Look Once)** for fast object detection
* Works with **high-resolution optical satellite imagery (RGB)**
* Demonstrates **end-to-end inference pipeline**
* Focused on **practical implementation for GeoAI analysts**

Ship detection in optical imagery is a core computer vision task in remote sensing, aiming to **localize and classify vessels in complex marine environments** [\[mdpi.com\]](https://www.mdpi.com/2072-4292/16/7/1145)

***

## 🧠 Methodology

### 🔍 Detection Approach

* Model: **YOLO-based object detector**
* Input: High-resolution optical imagery (RGB)
* Output:
  * Bounding boxes
  * Confidence scores
  * Ship detections

Unlike SAR-based approaches, this method relies on **visual features such as shape, texture, and contrast**, which requires robust deep learning models due to environmental complexity (waves, clouds, coastlines). [\[mdpi.com\]](https://www.mdpi.com/2072-4292/16/7/1145)

***

## 🧭 Tutorial Workflow

### 1. Data Preparation

* Load high-resolution satellite images
* Prepare RGB inputs for model inference
* Handle image resizing / normalization

### 2. Image Tiling (Optional for Large AOIs)

* Split large scenes into manageable tiles
* Ensure compatibility with YOLO input size

### 3. Model Setup

* Load pretrained YOLO weights
* Configure inference parameters

### 4. Deep Learning Inference

* Run YOLO model on images
* Generate ship detections

### 5. Post-processing

* Apply **confidence thresholding**
* Perform **Non-Max Suppression (NMS)**
* Remove duplicate detections

### 6. Visualization

* Overlay bounding boxes on RGB imagery
* Assess performance and edge cases

***

***

***

## ⚙️ Requirements

Typical environment:

```bash
Python >= 3.8
torch
ultralytics (YOLO)
opencv-python
numpy
matplotlib
```

Optional (recommended):

* GPU (CUDA) for faster inference
* Jupyter Notebook environment

***

## ▶️ How to Run

### 1. Clone the repository

```bash
git clone https://github.com/Tnecniv-Teikram/ship_detection_optical.git
cd ship_detection_optical
```

### 2. Install dependencies

```bash
pip install -r requirements.txt
```

### 3. Launch the notebook

```bash
jupyter notebook ship_detection.ipynb
```

***

## 📊 Output

The model produces:

* ✅ Bounding boxes around detected ships
* ✅ Confidence scores per detection
* ✅ Visual overlays on optical imagery

***

## ⚠️ Challenges in Optical Ship Detection

Compared to SAR, optical detection is more complex due to:

* 🌊 Waves and reflections
* ☁️ Cloud cover
* 🏝️ Coastal clutter (ports, infrastructure)
* 📏 Large variation in ship size

These challenges require **multi-scale deep learning models like YOLO to achieve reliable detection** [\[arxiv.org\]](https://arxiv.org/html/2512.09700v1)

***

## 📈 Applications

* Maritime traffic monitoring
* Port activity analysis
* Illegal fishing detection
* Coastal surveillance
* Environmental monitoring

***

## 🎓 Target Audience

This tutorial is designed for:

* **GeoAI analysts**
* Remote sensing specialists
* Data scientists working with EO data
* Space Academy participants / hackathon teams

***

## 🚀 Next Steps

To extend this workflow:

* Train YOLO on **custom ship datasets** (e.g., xView, ShipRSImageNet)
* Improve performance on **small vessels (tiny objects)**
* Integrate with **SAR workflows for multi-sensor fusion**
* Deploy as a **real-time monitoring pipeline**

***
